LIVE
Nov. 10, 2022, 11:41 a.m. ET

Congress Remains in Play as Georgia Senate Race Heads for Runoff

The runoff between Senator Raphael Warnock and Herschel Walker is set for December. The shape of Congress may not be clear until next week or beyond.

 

How Democrats Used the Abortion Debate to Hold Off a Red Wave

In the first major election post-Roe, Democrats tied abortion rights to everyday economics and tapped into fears about the rise of right-wing Republicans.

The political career of Florida’s governor, Ron DeSantis, has become supercharged.

With a House majority in reach, a new class of Republicans is taking shape.

Several key races have still not been called. Here’s where things stand.

In Texas, Democrats are looking to a future beyond Beto O’Rourke.

Former President Trump is under fire from within the Republican Party after the midterms.

U.S. Inflation Shows Signs of Cooling

LIVE
Nov. 10, 2022, 11:23 a.m. ET

October Report Suggests Fed’s Campaign Is Having an Effect

Consumer prices picked up by 7.7 percent in the year through October, lower than expected and a sign that inflation is beginning to moderate.

 

An Inflation-Driven Midterm Will Not Change Biden’s Economic Focus

President Biden said he would not change his economic approach, and that his policies were working.

Data showed that inflation remained rapid, but there were some signs of hope. Read takeaways from today’s report.

Markets soared, bolstering investors’ hopes that the Federal Reserve could slow the pace of interest rate rises.

LIVE
Nov. 10, 2022, 11:42 a.m. ET

Ukrainian Forces Advance After Russia Orders Kherson Retreat

Ukraine’s military said it had recaptured 12 settlements in the south, but it remained unclear if Russian forces were withdrawing from the city of Kherson.

 

Russia and Ukraine each have over 100,000 casualties, the top U.S. general said.

LIVE
Nov. 10, 2022, 11:29 a.m. ET

Tropical Storm Nicole Sweeps Across Florida

Nicole made landfall as a rare November hurricane before being downgraded to a tropical storm, prompting widespread power outages and coastal flooding.

 

The storm will move up the East Coast through the weekend, bringing heavy rain.

Biden and Xi Will Meet as Tensions Grow Over Taiwan

The meeting is an opportunity for face time for President Biden and Xi Jinping of China, but Mr. Biden said he would deliver no concessions over Taiwan.

Biden’s COP27 Climate Message Might Not Be the One the World Wants

President Biden is expected to hail the new U.S. climate law on Friday at the COP27 summit in Egypt. But other countries want to talk money.

‘Economic Picture Ahead Is Dire,’ Elon Musk Tells Twitter Employees

In his first communications with Twitter’s staff, the company’s new owner painted a bleak picture as more employees resigned.

FTX Crypto Exchange Boss Says He Is Trying to Raise More Money

Sam Bankman-Fried told employees that he was trying to reinforce his company’s finances days after it collapsed from a bank run.

Hiring the best, brightest and highest number of employees was a badge of honor in tech. Not anymore.

Employees of tech companies who were laid off are using social media to critique their former bosses.

NASA Launched an Inflatable Flying Saucer, With Mars in Mind

The LOFTID mission tested a novel approach to guiding spacecraft through the extreme heat of returning from orbit, which could be used to put people on Mars.

A Japanese American Family, a Native American Tribe and a Bountiful Friendship

In the face of discrimination and hate, the Inabas and the Yakama Nation forged a bond of more than 100 years through a farm in eastern Washington.

Female College Athletes Say Pressure to Cut Body Fat Is Toxic

Several women in track and field said their mental health spiraled as they skipped meals to be leaner at the urging of coaches and peers.

A museum’s worth of masterworks owned by the Microsoft co-founder were offered in a two-part charity sale. Five topped $100 million.
